notes: Current public development-line manifest for the 4.1.3.7 patch work after the 4.1.3.6 release-line AgentExecution slice, continuing preparation for the 4.1.4 AgentTask target.

runtime_event_ownership.plugin_contract: plugins return observations/errors/decisions; core maps them to official RuntimeEvent records

runtime_naming.agent_execution: run_kind for one AgentExecution-owned Agent run
runtime_naming.attempt_index: model-request retry attempt metadata; not an AgentExecution counter

runtime_capability_contract.skill_capability_needs: SkillsExecutor records selected Skill capability needs in SkillExecutionPlan.capability_needs from SKILL.md guidance, resource indexes, public compatibility, public metadata, and optional model-assisted inference.
runtime_capability_contract.search_provider: Framework Search uses the ddgs package by default, keeps backend configurable through host policy, supports host-approved ddgs refresh before search runs, treats no-result search as a successful empty result, and falls back through configured/default ddgs backends when a selected backend returns no usable parsed result.
runtime_capability_contract.workspace_file_action_owner: Workspace owns file action roots and path boundaries; SkillsExecutor prefers workspace.enable_file_actions(agent, ...) before falling back to agent.enable_workspace_file_actions(...).
runtime_capability_contract.policy_approval_surface: Skills capability approval uses the framework-wide PolicyApproval handler surface: input_timeout_fail by default, fail_closed for pending diagnostics/TriggerFlow gates, auto_approve for tests, input, or registered service-specific handlers.

action_runtime.partial_success_contract: Action results with status=partial_success keep success=True/ok=True and carry diagnostics for degraded providers or fallback recovery. ActionFlow treats partial_success as continuable evidence, not action.failed.
action_runtime.search_partial_success: Built-in Search returns partial_success when earlier ddgs backends fail or return empty parsed results but a later fallback backend returns usable results.
action_runtime.bash_sandbox_model_description: Model-visible bash sandbox action descriptions include the allowed command prefixes, allowed working-directory roots, and timeout when the action is registered or enabled through agent.enable_shell(...).

configuration.default_output_format: Omitted .output(..., format=...) reads prompt.default_output_format from the current settings chain; the global default is json, while explicit format="auto" and per-agent/request prompt.default_output_format="auto" remain available for schema-driven selection.
configuration.tuple_ensure_value_contract: Tuple ensure=True flags and runtime ensure_keys require path/key presence only: missing paths fail and share the normal retry budget, while None, blank strings, False, 0, empty lists, and other intentionally empty values remain valid when the path exists.
configuration.tuple_not_null_value_contract: Tuple ensure marker "not_null" requires the path/key to exist and resolve to a meaningful value: None, blank strings, empty lists or wildcard matches, and lists containing missing required values fail and share the normal retry budget; False and 0 remain valid typed values.

triggerflow.runtime_integrity_contract: TriggerFlow preserves execution-local runtime scope across framework-owned fan-out and chunk-internal emits; batch creates a real fan-out signal and isolates concurrent invocations; chunk-emitted signals carry parent signal identity and aggregation scope for DAG-friendly joins; flow.when([...], mode="and") is the event-list shorthand for multi-dependency joins; empty for_each emits an empty collected result; no-hit match restores its layer before continuing; pause_for(..., resume_to="self") records resume_count and defaults to max_resumes=1; pause_for(..., channel_id=..., provider_id=..., wait_mode=..., hot_wait_timeout=..., cold_persistence_policy=..., request_payload_schema=..., response_payload_schema=..., audit_metadata=...) records interrupt planned, persisted, and exposed phases plus a parameterized ExternalWait request envelope; runtime resource key execution_exchange_provider binds a host/plugin ExecutionExchangeProvider whose publish_request(...) can add exchange_id, audit_metadata, and provider_metadata after the interrupt is persisted and before it is exposed; provider publish failures mark the ExternalWait dispatch_state as exposure_failed and emit triggerflow.interrupt_exposure_failed; continue_with(..., resume_request_id=...) records resume accepted, dispatched, and completed/dispatch-failed phases, fails fast before acceptance when callbacks reach expired execution-local leases, and updates the ExternalWait dispatch state for idempotent callback replay; execution.save() includes a versioned top-level execution snapshot with durable TriggerFlow system progress, flow definition fingerprint validation, interrupt/resume ledger, ExternalWait request state, typed recovery diagnostics, resource requirements, lease metadata, TaskDAG graph fingerprint metadata, compaction segment metadata, retained lineage anchors, snapshot artifact refs, load read bounds, and managed execution environment requirements for restart-safe load without serializing live resources; Agently.create_trigger_flow(...) mirrors TriggerFlow(...) for factory-style flow definition creation; flow.create_execution(workspace=None) binds an execution-scoped lazy Workspace by default, workspace=False opts out, and workspace=shared_workspace/path/backend binds an application-owned Workspace through the execution-local runtime resource key workspace; flow/execution declare_resource_requirement(..., resolver=..., provider_kind=..., config_ref=..., secret_ref=..., fail_policy=...) persists importable resource resolver descriptors; execution.set_compaction_policy(...) forms the snapshot compaction surface; execution.inspect_load(...) reports missing resolver, unhealthy, policy-forbidden, pending resolver, unresolved resource, expired lease warnings, active lease owner conflicts, DAG join state mismatches, lineage-anchor mismatch, missing artifact, and invalid load-read-limit diagnostics; fail-closed pending resolvers or pending managed execution environments report status pending_resources and ready=false until async_load(...) resolves and validates live resources; execution.async_load(...) can rebuild live resources through importable resolvers before graph continuation; execution.inspect_runtime_event_records(...), execution.project_runtime_event_record(...), execution.async_save(..., expected_state_version=...), and idempotent continue_with(..., resume_request_id=...) form the load-facing contract; passing runtime_resources={"workspace": workspace} or runtime_resources={"durable_provider": provider} still binds compatible snapshot and RuntimeEvent provider ports automatically, but runtime_resources is only the process-local mount point for live objects already created, restored, and validated by the host; Workspace-backed snapshots are read through the provider and loaded by TriggerFlow for pause/continue, policy-approval waits, and when(..., mode="and") join progress; configured RuntimeEvent stores receive the same canonical RuntimeEvent identities that EventCenter dispatches plus durable projection fields for state_version, parent signal, aggregation scope, operator id, interrupt id, resume request id, actor id, exchange id, lease owner id, snapshot refs, and artifact refs; execution.async_save(..., require_distributed_provider=True) fails closed unless providers report CAS, lease, range-read, retention, and event-sequence capabilities and expose matching get_snapshot, lease, and artifact-ref methods; examples/trigger_flow/fastapi_sqlite_exchange_provider.py demonstrates a module-level TriggerFlow object with top-level .to(...) / .when(...) wiring, SQLite snapshot storage, a SQLite ExecutionExchangeProvider, and a FastAPI service boundary without claiming complete production distributed recovery; async_close runs once, clears transient aggregation scratch state, and removes closed executions from the live registry; execution concurrency is a global handler dispatch limit with permit yielding around awaited internal dispatch.

workspace.durable_provider_contract: Workspace remains the public durable information boundary and now exposes typed provider seams for stable reference envelopes, bounded reads, stream reads, RuntimeEvent storage, file-policy metadata, evidence links, retention anchors, checkpoint lookup, checkpoint CAS through put_checkpoint(..., expected_state_version=...), lease claim/heartbeat/release, artifact refs, backend-provider registration, and backend capability reporting. RuntimeEvent storage supports idempotency keys, expected_sequence fail-closed append checks, state_version, parent event and causation ids, parent signal id, aggregation scope, operator id, interrupt id, resume request id, actor id, lease owner id, snapshot refs, artifact refs, created_at, and persisted_at. Default Agents expose agent.workspace as a lazy Workspace foundation capability, and default TriggerFlow executions bind an execution-scoped lazy Workspace through runtime resource key workspace; the local filesystem plus SQLite backend materializes only when Workspace storage, checkpoint, evidence, read, or file-area behavior is actually used. Application-owned shared Workspaces are created through Workspace(...) or Agently.create_workspace(...) and explicitly bound to Agents, TriggerFlow executions, or service workers when they must share task information; separate Workspaces exchange information only through application-level search/read/write/link logic. Third-party Workspace backends can be passed directly to agent.use_workspace(backend), flow.create_execution(workspace=backend), or registered through Agently.workspace.register_backend_provider(name, factory) and selected with agent.use_workspace(root, provider=name, provider_options={...}); unregistered providers fail fast. TriggerFlow can bind compatible Workspace or durable-provider runtime resources to snapshot and RuntimeEvent provider ports automatically; provider snapshots can be read back and passed to TriggerFlow load for pause/continue, policy-approval waits, and when(..., mode="and") join progress. A protocol-level remote audit provider proof covers custom WorkspaceBackend replacement across Agent binding, provider registration, TriggerFlow snapshot/RuntimeEvent ports, distributed capability and method checks, lease methods, artifact refs, and AgentExecution evidence links. AgentExecution.async_record_workspace(...) writes requested checkpoints through the Workspace checkpoint-store port and records evidence links between AgentExecution records and checkpoints. The AgentTask task strategy writes its planning, observation, verification, checkpoint, and evidence-link records through Workspace provider ports while keeping strategy decisions in AgentExecution/AgentTask. Workspace does not own TriggerFlow execution policy, AgentExecution strategy selection, approval decisions, DAG readiness, DevTools projection truth, live resource serialization, or cross-space messaging/replication.
deferred: production external-provider distributed recovery operational guarantees
resumable AgentExecution strategies beyond the current AgentTask task-strategy evidence slice

agent_execution_request_scope.contract: Non-always Agent quick prompt calls create an isolated AgentExecution draft; AgentExecution owns execution-local ModelRequest prompt state and returns AgentExecutionResult. Prompt config .execution is the current one-run prompt section. AgentTurn, Agent.create_turn(...), Agent.set_turn_prompt(...), Agent.set_request_prompt(...), and AgentExecution.set_turn_prompt(...) are removed from the 4.1.3.7 development line. Agent-level persistent state remains on define(...), always=True, set_agent_prompt(...), and stable setup APIs.
agent_execution_request_scope.compatibility_policy: Expression-local chaining is the recommended execution-scoped shape. Multi-statement setup should capture execution = agent.create_execution() or use agent.input(...). Removed AgentTurn and turn/request prompt aliases are not compatibility surfaces for 4.1.3.7. Explicit low-level agent.create_request()/agent.request builders remain available for direct ModelRequest use.

response_stream_type_surface.contract: Common generator and handler type annotations are exposed through agently and agently.types.data. Result-named aliases are the recommended root-level public names: delta yields str, instant/streaming_parse yields StreamingData with is_complete, specific/all yield event-tagged result messages, AgentExecution streams yield AgentExecutionStreamData, and direct Skills stream handlers receive SkillRuntimeStreamItem dictionaries. Response-named aliases are compatibility-only aliases under agently.types.data and are not re-exported from the agently root.
response_stream_type_surface.compatibility_policy: StreamingData.is_complete remains the canonical instant/streaming_parse completion field. Response-named type aliases remain compatibility-only aliases in agently.types.data and are deprecated in favor of Result-named aliases; they will be removed in Agently 4.2.

model_response_result_facade.contract: get_result() is the recommended reusable facade for text, data, metadata, and streaming readers. Agent quick prompt chains return AgentExecutionResult; direct ModelRequest calls return ModelResponseResult. get_response() remains a compatibility alias. ModelResponseResult.result points to itself so older response.result reader chains continue to work.
model_response_result_facade.compatibility_policy: Recommended documentation, examples, and Skills guidance use result = agent.input(...).output(...).get_result(); data = result.get_data(). Code that already calls get_response() and then reader methods or response.result reader chains remains supported. Direct ModelResponse construction is deprecated and will be removed in Agently 4.2.

agent_execution_task_loop.contract: The 4.1.3.7 task-loop contract is a narrow single-task, single-Agent-owner strategy carried by AgentExecution. agent.goal(goal_or_goals, success_criteria=None), its plural alias agent.goals(...), agent.create_task(...), and agent.create_task_loop(...) return task-strategy AgentExecution drafts, not a separate recommended AgentTask execution owner. The second goal argument is the current success-criteria entrypoint; separate success_criteria(...) and success_standards(...) builders are not current public surfaces. effort(...) accepts preset names or a detailed strategy expansion for budget, planning, execution, verification, replan, and progress; effort budget iteration_limit maps to task-loop max_iterations, while model_call_limit and wall_time_seconds map to AgentExecution limits unless explicit limits were already set. effort execution.step_plan defaults to auto and can guide whether a Goal Pursuit iteration keeps one direct bounded step or may expand into a TaskDAG / DAG-shaped bounded step; DAG completion feeds evidence back to AgentTaskLoop and does not bypass model verification plus host guards. DynamicTask is the current compatibility/convenience facade over DAG planning and execution, not the strategic task lifecycle. AgentExecution.use_dynamic_task(...) adds an execution-local DAG candidate through that facade, while agent.use_dynamic_task(...) remains the Agent-level DAG candidate pool. Consumers read final data, text, streams, metadata, and task refs through AgentExecutionResult or the execution stream/meta facade. Completion acceptance requires model-owned verification plus conservative host guards; completed means accepted=true and artifact_status=accepted, blocked means accepted=false and artifact_status=blocked, while max_iterations can return accepted=false and artifact_status=partial. The task strategy writes Workspace checkpoints through the checkpoint-store port and records task evidence relationships with Workspace evidence links. AgentExecutionResult resume is reserved and reports supported=false until a resumable strategy lands.

current_slice: explicitly enabled Actions, Skills, or DAG candidates as step capabilities
model-owned planning, verification, and replan decisions
host guards for missing criteria, risky action evidence, approval-required actions, and missing final deliverables
structured task refs, diagnostics, progress/snapshot stream items, result facade consumption, and provider-backed Workspace evidence links
deferred: durable pause/resume beyond the reserved result surface

agent_execution_task_loop.compatibility_policy: New documentation and examples should keep AgentExecutionResult as the common consumption surface. Task-style compatibility readers may remain callable, but they should not be promoted as a second recommended public lifecycle. Future AgentTaskLoop hardening must strengthen the task strategy behind AgentExecution without changing this public ownership boundary.

image.attachment_contract: Builds text plus image_url rich-content blocks and keeps attachment as the low-level passthrough.
